rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KELLY KRISTINE SANDVIG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Ownership Of Estate Property And For Order Authorizing And Directing Administrator To Transfer Estate Property.
rulings.Rulings: continued to february 3, 2016 at counsel's request

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion For An Order Setting Aside And Vacating Order For An Inventory And An Accouting. Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Thereof, C.C.P. 473(B)&(D). Request For A Stay Of The November 10th Hearing Pending The Hearing On This Motion
rulings.Rulings: The motion is denied. To the extent it sought to stay the already-held November 10, 2105 hearing, it is moot, was procedurally inappropriate, and was substantively unsupported. To the extent it challenges the Court's jurisdiction or appointment of an administrator, those issues have been previously presented and decided, and Ms. Cohen has presented no persuasive basis to reconsider or to change the prior rulings. To the extent the motion seeks to stay or vacate the Court's order that she file an inventory of what she removed from Decedent's residence, CCP section 473(b) does not apply because the order is not a "judgment or dismissal" or "proceeding taking against her" without her input. Even if the section applied, Ms. Cohen has not convinced the Court that there is any basis for relief from the Order based on excusable neglect or fraud. Her arguments going to the merits of the ultimate distribution of property from the estate have not been relevant so far because the Court has not made any determination as to who is entitled to the estate's property or even as to what property belongs to the estate. It has so far only required her to supply information in her possession necessary to making those determinations. She has had every opportunity to provide her input as to the orders issued so far. The pending petition seeking a turnover of property has not been decided, and the time to object to it has not yet run--though it is on calendar for hearing December 15, 2015 along with other matters set out in the November 20, 2015 Order to Show Cause. She remains free to file objections or a response to that petition until that date. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the other side and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, December 8, 2015.
